title: paspalum sanguinale var. pruriens
intro: Paspalum sanguinale var. pruriens, also known as itchgrass, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Paspalum sanguinale var. pruriens include its ability to relieve itching and irritation, reduce fever, and treat various skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in the form of a paste or ointment, to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
